title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

seo.title: Yangnyeom Chicken Recipe: Authentic Korean Fried Chicken
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Yangnyeom Chicken with our easy recipe! Get crispy Korean fried chicken coated in the perfect sweet & spicy sauce. Make the best KFC at home!
h2Headings: How to Make Yangnyeom Chicken: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Crispy Yangnyeom Chicken
Serving Suggestions for Your Korean Fried Chicken
seo.imageAlt: Plate of crispy Yangnyeom Chicken, Korean fried chicken coated in sweet and spicy sauce, garnished with sesame seeds.
seo.imageCaption: Our Yangnyeom Chicken recipe delivers irresistible crispy texture and that iconic sweet and spicy sauce right to your kitchen!

description: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved dish featuring incredibly crispy fried chicken pieces coated in a deliciously addictive sweet, spicy, and tangy sauce. This iconic Korean comfort food is famous for its perfect balance of flavors and textures, making it a favorite worldwide. Enjoy making this restaurant-quality dish at home, perfect for a gathering or a satisfying meal.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skin-on chicken thighs or drumsticks

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp minced ginger, 1 tsp minced garlic, mirin, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/4 tsp black pepper. Mix well to coat all chicken pieces. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: In a separate large bowl, whisk together the potato starch, all-purpose flour, and baking powder. Add the cold water and egg, then whisk until a smooth, thick batter forms. Do not overmix; a few lumps are fine. The batter should be similar to pancake batter consistency.

instructions.title: First Fry the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 4-5 cups of vegetable oil in a deep pot or Dutch oven to 170°C (340°F). Dip each marinated chicken piece into the batter, ensuring it is fully coated, and let any excess drip off. Carefully place chicken pieces into the hot oil, frying in batches to avoid overcrowding the pot. Fry for 6-7 minutes until light golden brown and cooked through. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a wire rack to drain.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: While the chicken is frying, prepare the sauce. In a medium saucepan, combine gochujang, gochugaru, 2 tbsp soy sauce, rice vinegar, brown sugar, corn syrup (or honey), 1 tbsp minced garlic, 1/2 tsp minced ginger, sesame oil, and 3 tbsp water. Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat, stirring constantly until the sugar is dissolved and the sauce thickens slightly, about 3-5 minutes. Remove from heat.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Crispy Perfection
instructions.text: Increase the oil temperature to 185°C (365°F). Return the first-fried chicken pieces to the hot oil in batches. Fry for another 2-3 minutes, or until the chicken is deeply golden brown and extra crispy. This double-frying step is crucial for achieving the signature crunchy texture of Korean fried chicken. Remove and drain on a wire rack.

instructions.title: Coat and Serve
instructions.text: Once all chicken is double-fried, transfer the hot chicken to a large mixing bowl. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and toss gently but quickly until every piece is evenly coated. Garnish with roasted sesame seeds, chopped peanuts (if using), and sliced green onions. Serve immediately and enjoy your authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken!

tips: Double-frying the chicken is the secret to achieving the incredibly crispy texture characteristic of authentic Korean fried chicken. Do not skip this step!
Adjust the amount of gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) in the sauce to control the spice level. For a milder version, reduce or omit it; for extra heat, add more.
Serve Yangnyeom Chicken immediately after coating to enjoy its optimal crispiness. While leftovers are still delicious, they may lose some crunch upon reheating.
article:
Dive into the irresistible world of Korean Fried Chicken (KFC), a culinary phenomenon that has captivated taste buds far beyond the streets of Seoul. While many variations exist, one stands out for its perfect balance of sweet, spicy, and savory flavors, all coating an impossibly crispy piece of fried chicken: Yangnyeom Chicken. This beloved dish is more than just fried chicken; it's an experience, a staple of Korean food culture often enjoyed with friends, family, and a refreshing drink. If you've ever craved that unique crunch followed by an explosion of flavor, you're in the right place to learn all about this iconic Korean chicken recipe.

## What is Yangnyeom Chicken?
"Yangnyeom" (양념) literally translates to "seasoned" in Korean, and that's precisely what sets Yangnyeom Chicken apart. Unlike its American counterpart, which often relies on a simple salt and pepper dredge, Korean fried chicken, particularly Yangnyeom Chicken, is known for its delicate, super-crispy crust and a vibrant, flavorful sauce. This sauce is a complex blend of gochujang (Korean chili paste), garlic, soy sauce, sugar or syrup, and vinegar, creating a symphony of tastes that is both addictive and incredibly satisfying. The chicken itself is typically double-fried, a technique crucial for achieving that signature airy crispness that holds up remarkably well even after being tossed in the rich, glossy sauce. It’s a dish that embodies the dynamic and bold flavors of Korean cuisine, making it a global favorite.

## Ingredients for Korean Yangnyeom Chicken
Crafting authentic Yangnyeom Chicken requires a few key ingredients, some of which are distinctly Korean but readily available in Asian supermarkets or online.

For the Chicken and Coating:
*   **Chicken:** 2-3 lbs chicken pieces (wings, drumsticks, or boneless thighs work best).
*   **Marinade (optional but recommended):** 1 tsp grated ginger, 2 cloves minced garlic, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp mirin (rice wine).
*   **Dry Coating:** 1 cup potato starch (or cornstarch), 1/2 cup all-purpose flour, 1 tsp baking powder, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp black pepper.
*   **Frying Oil:** Enough neutral oil with a high smoke point (canola, vegetable, peanut) to submerge the chicken pieces.

For the Yangnyeom Sauce:
*   **Gochujang:** 3-4 tbsp (Korean chili paste – adjust to your spice preference).
*   **Soy Sauce:** 2 tbsp.
*   **Sugar/Syrup:** 2 tbsp corn syrup, rice syrup, or honey (for sweetness and a glossy finish).
*   **Vinegar:** 1 tbsp rice vinegar.
*   **Garlic:** 3-4 cloves, minced.
*   **Ginger:** 1/2 tsp grated (optional).
*   **Gochugaru:** 1 tsp (Korean chili flakes – optional, for extra heat and color).
*   **Sesame Oil:** 1 tsp.
*   **Water or Chicken Broth:** 2-3 tbsp (to thin the sauce).
*   **Optional Garnish:** Toasted sesame seeds, chopped peanuts, sliced scallions.

## How to Make Yangnyeom Chicken: Step-by-Step
Making perfect Yangnyeom Chicken at home is a rewarding process. Follow these steps for crispy, flavorful results:

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Pat your chicken pieces thoroughly dry with paper towels. If marinating, combine the marinade ingredients and toss with the chicken. Let sit for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator. After marinating, pat dry again to remove excess moisture.
2.  **Coat the Chicken:** In a large bowl, whisk together the potato starch, flour, baking powder, salt, and pepper. Dredge each piece of chicken in the dry mixture, ensuring it's fully coated. Shake off any excess.
3.  **First Fry:** Heat your frying oil in a large pot or deep fryer to 325°F (160°C). Carefully add the chicken pieces in batches, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for 6-8 minutes, until lightly golden and cooked through. Remove the chicken and place it on a wire rack to drain and cool for at least 10-15 minutes. This cooling period is crucial for achieving the double-fry crispiness.
4.  **Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce:** While the chicken cools, combine all Yangnyeom sauce ingredients (gochujang, soy sauce, syrup, vinegar, garlic, ginger, gochugaru, sesame oil, water/broth) in a small saucepan. Bring to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens slightly and becomes glossy (about 3-5 minutes). Remove from heat.
5.  **Second Fry:** Increase the oil temperature to 375°F (190°C). Return the cooled chicken to the hot oil for a second fry. Fry for another 3-5 minutes, or until deep golden brown and exceptionally crispy. Remove and place on a wire rack.
6.  **Sauce and Serve:** Immediately after the second fry, transfer the hot, crispy chicken to a large bowl. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and toss gently but quickly to coat every piece evenly. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds, chopped peanuts, or scallions if desired. Serve immediately.

## Tips for the Best Crispy Yangnyeom Chicken
Achieving that signature crispiness and perfect flavor balance in your Yangnyeom Chicken isn't difficult with these pro tips:

*   **Pat Dry, Always:** Moisture is the enemy of crispiness. Ensure your chicken is as dry as possible before coating.
*   **The Right Starch:** Potato starch is often preferred for Korean fried chicken as it creates a lighter, crispier crust than cornstarch, though cornstarch is a good substitute.
*   **Don't Skip the Double Fry:** This is non-negotiable for authentic KFC crispiness. The first fry cooks the chicken, and the second fry, at a higher temperature, creates that incredibly crunchy, airy crust.
*   **Mind the Oil Temperature:** Use a thermometer to monitor your oil. Too low, and the chicken will be greasy; too high, and it will burn before cooking through.
*   **Don't Overcrowd:** Frying too many pieces at once lowers the oil temperature, leading to soggy chicken. Fry in batches.
*   **Sauce at the Last Minute:** Toss the chicken in the sauce right before serving. The sauce is meant to cling to the crispy exterior, not soak into it. Don't let the chicken sit in the sauce for too long, or it will lose its crunch.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Korean Fried Chicken
Yangnyeom Chicken is a star in its own right, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Korean accompaniments.

*   **Pickled Radish (Mu Saengchae):** The tangy, refreshing crunch of pickled radish is the classic palate cleanser for rich, saucy fried chicken.
*   **Cold Beer or Soju:** In Korea, "Chimaek" (chicken and beer) is a beloved combination. A cold lager or a shot of soju cuts through the richness of the chicken beautifully.
*   **Steamed Rice:** A bowl of fluffy white rice provides a neutral base to soak up any extra sauce.
*   **Kimchi:** No Korean meal is complete without kimchi, offering a fermented, spicy, and sour counterpoint.
*   **Coleslaw or Corn Salad:** Creamy coleslaw or a sweet corn salad can offer a refreshing textural and flavor contrast.

Whether it's for a casual dinner, a game night, or a lively gathering, Yangnyeom Chicken is sure to be a crowd-pleaser.

## Yangnyeom Sauce Variations & Substitutions
While the classic Yangnyeom sauce is perfect, you can easily customize it to suit your taste or dietary needs.

*   **Adjust Spice Levels:** For less heat, reduce the gochujang and omit the gochugaru. For more fire, increase both!
*   **Sweetness Control:** Experiment with different sweeteners like honey, brown sugar, or even a touch of fruit juice for varied flavor profiles.
*   **Tangy Twist:** A squeeze of fresh lemon or lime juice can add an extra layer of brightness to the sauce.
*   **Garlic-Honey Variation:** Reduce gochujang and add more honey and minced garlic for a sweeter, less spicy honey-garlic Yangnyeom Chicken.
*   **Soy Garlic:** For those who prefer a non-spicy option, a soy-garlic sauce made with soy sauce, sugar, garlic, and a touch of mirin is a fantastic alternative.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Use tamari instead of soy sauce and ensure your dry coating is made with a gluten-free flour blend.
*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** The sauce can be used to coat crispy fried tofu, cauliflower florets, or mushrooms for a plant-based take on this flavorful dish.

Embrace the joy of making and sharing Yangnyeom Chicken. This iconic Korean fried chicken recipe is a testament to the country's vibrant culinary heritage, offering a delicious and memorable experience with every crispy, saucy bite.
